content:
# 2 Thessalonians Chapter 1 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF page 204 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2TH_001_page_001.jpg

---

**1:1** Paul and Silvanus and Timothy, to the assembly [ekklesia] of-Thessalonians in God our Father and [the] Lord Jesus Christ:

**1:2** grace to-you and peace from God [the] Father and [the] Lord Jesus Christ.

**1:3** We-ought to-give-thanks to-God always concerning you, brothers, just-as [it] is worthy, because your faith grows-exceedingly, and the love of-each one of-all of-you toward one-another abounds,

**1:4** so-that we ourselves boast in you in the assemblies [ekklesiais] of-God, concerning your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and the afflictions which you-bear,

**1:5** [which is] a-manifest-token of-the righteous judgment of-God, for you to-be-counted-worthy of-the kingdom of-God, for which you also suffer,

**1:6** since-indeed [it is] righteous before God to-repay to-the-[ones]-afflicting you, affliction,

**1:7** and to-you, the-[ones]-being-afflicted, rest with us, at the revelation of-the Lord Jesus from heaven with [the] angels of-his power,

**1:8** in flaming fire, giving vengeance to-the-[ones] not knowing God and to-the-[ones] not obeying the good-news of-our Lord Jesus,

**1:9** who will-pay [the] penalty [of] eternal destruction from [the] face of-the Lord and from the glory of-his strength,

**1:10** when he-comes to-be-glorified in his saints and to-be-marveled-at in all the-[ones]-having-believed, because our testimony toward you was-believed, in that day.

**1:11** For which also we-pray always concerning you, that our God may-count you worthy of-the calling, and may-fulfill every good-pleasure of-goodness and [every] work of-faith in power,

**1:12** so-that the name of-our Lord Jesus may-be-glorified in you, and you in him, according-to the grace of-our God and [the] Lord Jesus Christ.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **1:1 — "Silvanus"**: Codex Sinaiticus reads CIΛΟΥΑΝΟC (Silouanos). This is the same individual called "Silas" in Acts. The Latinized form "Silvanus" is retained as it appears in the epistolary form.

2. **1:2 — "grace to you and peace from God the Father"**: Sinaiticus reads the full form ΧΑΡΙC ΥΜΙΝ ΚΑΙ ΕΙΡΗΝΗ ΑΠΟ ΘΥ ΠΑΤΡΟC ΚΑΙ ΚΥ ΙΥ ΧΥ (with nomina sacra). Note that 1 Thessalonians in Sinaiticus omits the "from God our Father" expansion, but here in 2 Thessalonians it is present. Some witnesses read "God our Father" (ΘΕΟΥ ΠΑΤΡΟC ΗΜΩΝ) with the pronoun; Sinaiticus reads ΘΥ ΠΡC — the abbreviated form appears without ΗΜΩΝ ("our") in the greeting formula of v. 2, though "our" (ΗΜΩΝ) does appear with "Father" in v. 1.

3. **1:3 — "we ought" (opheilomen)**: ΟΦΕΙΛΟΜΕΝ — this is distinctive to 2 Thessalonians. Where 1 Thess 1:2 uses the simple "we give thanks," here Paul uses "we are obligated/we ought to give thanks," suggesting a sense of moral duty or theological necessity in the thanksgiving.

4. **1:3 — "grows exceedingly" (hyperauxanei)**: ΥΠΕΡΑΥΞΑΝΕΙ — a compound verb found only here in the NT. The prefix ΥΠΕΡ- ("over, beyond") intensifies ΑΥΞΑΝΩ ("to grow"), producing "grows beyond measure" or "super-grows." Rendered "grows-exceedingly" to capture the intensification.

5. **1:5 — "a manifest token" (endeigma)**: ΕΝΔΕΙΓΜΑ — this noun occurs only here in the NT (distinct from the more common ΕΝΔΕΙΞΙC). It denotes a proof, evidence, or clear indicator. The sentence structure is compressed: the demonstrative token points backward to the persecutions and forward to God's righteous judgment.

6. **1:7 — "rest" (anesin)**: ΑΝΕCΙΝ — literally "a loosening, a release." The word implies relief from tension or pressure, paired antithetically with "affliction" (ΘΛΙΨΙΝ) in v. 6. Rendered "rest" in the sense of respite or relief.

7. **1:8 — "in flaming fire"**: Sinaiticus reads ΕΝ ΠΥΡΙ ΦΛΟΓΟC (en pyri phlogos — "in fire of flame"). Some manuscripts reverse the order to ΕΝ ΦΛΟΓΙ ΠΥΡΟC ("in flame of fire"). The Sinaiticus reading places "fire" as the primary noun with "of flame" as the genitive qualifier.

8. **1:9 — "eternal destruction" (olethron aionion)**: ΟΛΕΘΡΟΝ ΑΙΩΝΙΟΝ — the word ΟΛΕΘΡΟC denotes ruin or destruction (cf. 1 Thess 5:3), not annihilation in the philosophical sense. ΑΙΩΝΙΟΝ ("age-long, eternal") qualifies the duration. The phrase "from the face of the Lord" (ΑΠΟ ΠΡΟCΩΠΟΥ ΤΟΥ ΚΥ) could be read as separation ("away from the presence") or as source ("proceeding from the presence"). Both senses may be operative; the Greek is ambiguous and has been preserved without resolution.

9. **1:10 — "was believed" (episteuthe)**: ΕΠΙCΤΕΥΘΗ — aorist passive of ΠΙCΤΕΥΩ. The parenthetical clause "because our testimony toward you was-believed" (ΟΤΙ ΕΠΙCΤΕΥΘΗ ΤΟ ΜΑΡΤΥΡΙΟΝ ΗΜΩΝ ΕΦ ΥΜΑC) interrupts the sentence between "all the ones having believed" and "in that day." This awkward construction is preserved as it appears in the Greek.

10. **1:11 — "good pleasure of goodness" (eudokian agathosynes)**: ΕΥΔΟΚΙΑΝ ΑΓΑΘΩCΥΝΗC — a dense genitive construction. ΕΥΔΟΚΙΑ means "good pleasure, delight, desire" and ΑΓΑΘΩCΥΝΗ means "goodness, uprightness." The phrase could mean "every desire for goodness" (objective genitive) or "every good resolve produced by goodness" (subjective genitive). Left unresolved in translation.

11. **1:12 — "our God and Lord Jesus Christ"**: The Greek ΤΟΥ ΘΥ ΗΜΩΝ ΚΑΙ ΚΥ ΙΥ ΧΥ (with nomina sacra) could be read as one referent under the Granville Sharp rule ("our God-and-Lord Jesus Christ") or as two referents ("our God, and [the] Lord Jesus Christ"). The single article ΤΟΥ governs the construction. Both readings have been proposed; the translation preserves the ambiguity with "our God and [the] Lord Jesus Christ."

12. **General scribal note**: The text of 2 Thessalonians in Sinaiticus follows immediately after 1 Thessalonians with no major spacing or decoration. Nomina sacra in this chapter include: ΘΥ/ΘΩ (God), ΚΥ/ΚΩ (Lord), ΙΥ (Jesus), ΧΥ (Christ), ΟΥΝΟΥ/ΟΥΝΟΙC (heaven/s), ΠΝΕΥΜΑΤΟC is written in full in nearby contexts. The scribe uses standard Sinaiticus orthographic conventions throughout.

--- EMET
